udt_struct_v0.doc: Price data returned from batch-capable adapters (RedStone, Pyth, Switchboard, etc.)
udt_struct_v0.name: AdapterPriceData

udt_union_v0.doc:
Instance storage keys for bounded configuration data.

Instance storage is used only for bounded configuration that doesn't grow
with the number of assets. Dynamic per-asset data is stored in persistent storage.
udt_union_v0.name: InstanceKey

void_v0.doc: TTL in seconds for cached price data. 0 = disabled.
void_v0.name: PriceCacheTtl

udt_union_v0.doc:
Persistent storage keys for dynamic per-asset data.

Persistent storage is used for unbounded data that grows with the number of assets,
with per-key TTL to avoid size cap issues and shared archival problems.
udt_union_v0.name: PersistentKey

tuple_v0.doc: Asset configuration for a specific asset
tuple_v0.name: AssetConfig

void_v0.doc: List of all whitelisted assets (for iteration)
void_v0.name: AssetList

tuple_v0.doc: Circuit breaker: stores last validated price per asset to detect anomalous price movements
tuple_v0.name: LastPrice

tuple_v0.doc: Public mirror of the last validated price. Public reads may renew this\nentry's TTL without keeping the circuit-breaker baseline alive.
tuple_v0.name: PublicLastPrice

tuple_v0.doc: Cached full PriceData per asset for TTL-based cache
tuple_v0.name: LastPriceData

tuple_v0.doc: Source-specific custom oracle metadata for a specific asset.
tuple_v0.name: CustomOracleMetadata

tuple_v0.doc: Source-specific batch oracle metadata for a specific asset.
tuple_v0.name: BatchOracleMetadata

udt_struct_v0.doc: Cached price data with the ledger timestamp when it was cached.
udt_struct_v0.name: CachedPriceData

fields.doc: Ledger timestamp when this entry was cached
fields.name: cached_at

udt_struct_v0.doc: Metadata that applies to one configured oracle source only.
udt_struct_v0.name: OracleSourceMetadata

function_v0.doc:
Accept admin role (two-step transfer, step 2).
Only the pending admin can call this to finalize the transfer.

# Arguments
* `caller` - Pending admin address (must be authorized)

# Errors
* `InvalidCalculation` - No pending admin proposal or caller is not the pending admin
function_v0.name: accept_admin

function_v0.doc:
Propose a new admin address (two-step transfer, step 1).
Only the current admin can propose a new admin.
The proposed admin must call `accept_admin` to complete the transfer.

# Arguments
* `caller` - Current admin address (must be authorized)
* `pending_admin` - Proposed new admin address

# Errors
* `Unauthorized` - Caller is not current admin
function_v0.name: propose_admin

function_v0.doc:
Get last known price for an asset (for debugging and monitoring).

Returns a public mirror of the stored price, or None if no price has
been recorded yet for this asset.
function_v0.name: get_last_price

function_v0.doc:
Force-refresh cached prices for specific assets.
Clears cached price data and fetches fresh prices from external sources.
Use this before budget-sensitive operations (liquidation, swap_collateral)
to ensure the cache is warm and subsequent calls hit the cache.
function_v0.name: refresh_prices

function_v0.doc: Configure a batch-capable oracle adapter for direct queries.\nThe adapter must implement: read_price_data_for_feed(String) and read_price_data(Vec<String>)\nreturning {price: U256, package_timestamp: u64, write_timestamp: u64}.
function_v0.name: set_batch_oracle

function_v0.doc: Get custom oracle address for a specific asset
function_v0.name: get_custom_oracle

function_v0.doc:
Get the pending admin address, if any.

# Returns
* `Ok(Address)` - Pending admin address
* `Err(InvalidCalculation)` - No pending admin proposal exists
function_v0.name: get_pending_admin

function_v0.doc: Set a custom oracle for an asset. The oracle must implement:\n- lastprice(asset: Asset) -> Option<PriceData>\n- decimals() -> u32  (`decimals` param, when provided, must match live value)
function_v0.name: set_custom_oracle

function_v0.doc:
Sets a manual price override for emergency situations.
Requires an expiry timestamp to prevent permanent mispricing.

# Arguments
* `caller` - Admin address (must be authorized)
* `asset` - Asset to override price for
* `price` - Override price (None to remove override)
* `expiry_timestamp` - Expiry timestamp in seconds (None to remove override, required when setting price)
function_v0.name: set_manual_override

function_v0.doc: Set the TTL (in seconds) for the price cache. 0 = disabled.
function_v0.name: set_price_cache_ttl

function_v0.doc:
Cancel a pending admin proposal.
Only the current admin can cancel a pending proposal.

# Arguments
* `caller` - Current admin address (must be authorized)

# Errors
* `Unauthorized` - Caller is not current admin
* `InvalidCalculation` - No pending admin proposal exists
function_v0.name: cancel_admin_proposal

function_v0.doc:
Reset circuit breaker for a specific asset (admin only).

Clears the stored last known price, allowing the next price query to bypass
the circuit breaker check. Use this when legitimate large price movements
occur (e.g., major market events, token migrations, or oracle upgrades).
function_v0.name: reset_circuit_breaker

function_v0.doc:
Reset circuit breaker for all assets (admin only).

Emergency function to clear all stored last known prices. Use sparingly
and only when necessary, as it temporarily disables circuit breaker protection
for all assets until new prices are queried.
function_v0.name: reset_all_circuit_breakers

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Error conditions for upgradeable contract operations.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: UpgradeError

fields.doc: Batch-capable adapter address (any oracle implementing read_price_data interface)
fields.name: batch_adapter

fields.doc: Feed identifier for the batch adapter (e.g. "BTC", "ETH")
fields.name: feed_id

fields.doc: Maximum age in seconds for custom/batch oracle prices (None = use global staleness threshold)
fields.name: max_age

fields.doc: Cached decimals for the oracle source \xe2\x80\x94 skips the decimals() cross-contract call when set
fields.name: oracle_decimals

fields.doc: Unix timestamp in seconds (matching env.ledger().timestamp()) when manual override expires
fields.name: override_expiry_timestamp

fields.doc: Unix timestamp when the manual override was set (returned as PriceData.timestamp\nso downstream staleness checks detect stale overrides). H-01 fix.
fields.name: override_set_timestamp

fields.doc: Circuit breaker: max price change between consecutive queries in basis points.\nDefault: 2000 = 20%. Prevents oracle failures from causing extreme price jumps.\nSet to 0 to disable. See L-8 security audit finding.
fields.name: max_price_change_bps

udt_struct_v0.doc: Internal debt tracking during flash loan execution
udt_struct_v0.name: FlashLoanDebt

fields.doc: Flash loan fee in basis points (e.g., 30 = 0.3%)
fields.name: fee_bps

fields.doc: Flash loan premium to protocol (vs LP suppliers)
fields.name: premium_to_protocol

fields.doc: Flash loan premium percentage (total to protocol)
fields.name: premium_total

fields.doc: Interest rate modes (0 = no open debt, 1 = variable)
fields.name: modes

fields.doc: User on whose behalf the flash loan is taken
fields.name: on_behalf_of

fields.doc: Additional parameters for flash loan callback
fields.name: params

fields.doc: Borrow cap in whole tokens (e.g., 500000 = 500K tokens)\nWhen checking caps, multiply by 10^decimals to get smallest units
fields.name: borrow_cap

fields.doc: Number of decimals for the asset
fields.name: decimals

fields.doc: Loan to value ratio (in basis points)
fields.name: ltv

fields.doc: Supply cap in whole tokens (e.g., 1000000 = 1M tokens)\nWhen checking caps, multiply by 10^decimals to get smallest units
fields.name: supply_cap

fields.doc: Maximum debt ceiling for isolated asset
fields.name: debt_ceiling

fields.doc: Current total debt for isolated asset
fields.name: total_debt

fields.doc: Amount of collateral seized from user
fields.name: collateral_seized

fields.doc: Debt asset address (needed for profit distribution)
fields.name: debt_asset

fields.doc: Liquidator's profit after covering debt + fees
fields.name: profit

udt_struct_v0.doc: Bitmap: each pair of bits = [collateral, borrowed] for reserve index
udt_struct_v0.name: UserConfiguration

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Bitmap layout:
data_low: LTV (0-13), liquidation_threshold (14-27), liquidation_bonus (28-41),
decimals (42-49), flags (50-56), reserve_factor (57-70)
data_high: borrow_cap (0-63), supply_cap (64-127)
udt_struct_v0.name: ReserveConfiguration

fields.doc: Whether to receive aToken or underlying asset
fields.name: receive_a_token

udt_struct_v0.doc: Liquidation callback parameters for flash loan-based liquidation
udt_struct_v0.name: LiquidationCallbackParams

fields.doc: Collateral price from oracle (validated at call time)
fields.name: collateral_price

fields.doc: Collateral reserve data (cached to avoid re-reads)
fields.name: collateral_reserve_data

fields.doc: Debt price from oracle (validated at call time)
fields.name: debt_price

fields.doc: Debt reserve data (cached to avoid re-reads)
fields.name: debt_reserve_data

fields.doc: Minimum swap output for slippage protection
fields.name: min_swap_out

fields.doc: Optional swap handler for DEX-agnostic swaps
fields.name: swap_handler

udt_struct_v0.doc: Flash liquidation validation parameters\nPassed to helper contract to reduce parameter count
udt_struct_v0.name: FlashLiquidationValidationParams

udt_struct_v0.doc: Flash liquidation validation result\nReturned by the flash liquidation helper contract
udt_struct_v0.name: FlashLiquidationValidationResult

cases.doc: LOW-3: Partial repay would leave dust debt below min_remaining_debt
cases.name: RepayWouldLeaveDust

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: L-13\nReplaces raw panic!() calls for on-chain debuggability.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: ConfigurationError
